visa_types.visa_fees:
[ita > phl > en > Table:Seafarer- Transit ( C ) > Visa Fees 1]
- In addition to the fees paid for the visa, an additional VFS charge of PHP 1933 (including VAT) is applied for each visa application.
- All Fees would be collected at the application submission counter by CASH and Card Payments .

Note: In case of failure to grant the visa, the amount paid is not under any circumstances refundable. 
- Applicants must pay a visa fee for the equivalent of € 90 in local currency Philippine Peso at the exchange rate set by the Embassy that can change periodically.
visa_types.forms:
SCHENGEN VISA APPLICAATION FOR English
SCHENGEN VISA APPLICAATION FORM  Italian
visa_types.overview:
Overview
- For foreign overseas workers who are to embark ships carrying an Italian flag, please subject to the provisions relating to transit visas, and for contractual employees of foreign companies recruited to embark on Italian cruise ship for complementary services (referred to article 17 of
the law 5 December
1986, n. 856), take note that the specific dispositions of the law that respects the matter are to be observed and an authorization to work is not necessary. Furthermore, they permit permanent boarding in the ship, even if the same ship sails in territorial seas or docks in a national port.
- The foreign overseas workers, who intend to disembark from a ship carrying an Italian or foreign flag in national zones or around the Schengen area, need to leave the transit visa in the border with the presence of the ship to the responsible captain of the Italian port. In case the disembarkation of the foreign worker is within Italian territory, the general rules on entry and residence applies basing on the Testo Unico sull'Immigrazione.
- This visa is given for a period of time of less than 90 days.
BIOMETRIC DATA 
Following the entry into force of Decree-Law 145/2024, as of 11 January 2025, it will be mandatory to collect the fingerprints of applicants for both Schengen visas (type C) and national entry visas (type D). Applicants will therefore be required to appear in person when submitting their visa application. This is a simple procedure that takes only a few minutes. Since biometric data will be stored for 59 months, applicants will not need to provide fingerprints again for any subsequent visa applications, and it will not be necessary to apply in person for future visa requests.
Exemptions from the obligation to provide fingerprints are granted to a limited number of applicants: children under the age of 12 and individuals for whom fingerprint collection is physically impossible. Heads of State, government members, and accompanying delegation members, as well as spouses, are also exempted when attending official visits.
PROCESSING TIMES FOR VISA APPLICATIONS
Schengen regulations provide for a visa processing time of 15 calendar days, which may be extended up to 30 days if necessary (Art. 23 of the Schengen Visa Code). For national visas, processing time may extend to 30 days for family and employment purposes, and up to 120 days for self-employment. The Embassy of Italy in Manila is committed to processing each visa application as efficiently as possible.
Please note that, especially during the period from February to September, applicants are strongly advised to observe the minimum timeframe of 15 calendar days between the submission of the application and the intended date of travel, as applications submitted with a shorter notice may not be processed in time.
Applications may be submitted as early as 180 days before the intended date of travel (270 days for seafarers).Additional processing may apply to applicants who are nationals of countries subject to security consultations.
You may visit the Italian Embassy in Manila (https://ambmanila.esteri.it/en/servizi-consolari-e-visti/servizi-per-il-cittadino-straniero/visti/) website for more information about processing of visa applications.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
- For foreign overseas workers who are to embark ships carrying an Italian flag, please subject to the provisions relating to transit visas, and for contractual employees of foreign companies recruited to embark on Italian cruise ship for complementary services (referred to article 17 of
the law 5 December
1986, n. 856), take note that the specific dispositions of the law that respects the matter are to be observed and an authorization to work is not necessary. Furthermore, they permit permanent boarding in the ship, even if the same ship sails in territorial seas or docks in a national port.
- The foreign overseas workers, who intend to disembark from a ship carrying an Italian or foreign flag in national zones or around the Schengen area, need to leave the transit visa in the border with the presence of the ship to the responsible captain of the Italian port. In case the disembarkation of the foreign worker is within Italian territory, the general rules on entry and residence applies basing on the Testo Unico sull'Immigrazione.
- This visa is given for a period of time of less than 90 days.
BIOMETRIC DATA 
Following the entry into force of Decree-Law 145/2024, as of 11 January 2025, it will be mandatory to collect the fingerprints of applicants for both Schengen visas (type C) and national entry visas (type D). Applicants will therefore be required to appear in person when submitting their visa application. This is a simple procedure that takes only a few minutes. Since biometric data will be stored for 59 months, applicants will not need to provide fingerprints again for any subsequent visa applications, and it will not be necessary to apply in person for future visa requests.
Exemptions from the obligation to provide fingerprints are granted to a limited number of applicants: children under the age of 12 and individuals for whom fingerprint collection is physically impossible. Heads of State, government members, and accompanying delegation members, as well as spouses, are also exempted when attending official visits.
PROCESSING TIMES FOR VISA APPLICATIONS
Schengen regulations provide for a visa processing time of 15 calendar days, which may be extended up to 30 days if necessary (Art. 23 of the Schengen Visa Code). For national visas, processing time may extend to 30 days for family and employment purposes, and up to 120 days for self-employment. The Embassy of Italy in Manila is committed to processing each visa application as efficiently as possible.
Please note that, especially during the period from February to September, applicants are strongly advised to observe the minimum timeframe of 15 calendar days between the submission of the application and the intended date of travel, as applications submitted with a shorter notice may not be processed in time.
Applications may be submitted as early as 180 days before the intended date of travel (270 days for seafarers).Additional processing may apply to applicants who are nationals of countries subject to security consultations.
You may visit the Italian Embassy in Manila (https://ambmanila.esteri.it/en/servizi-consolari-e-visti/servizi-per-il-cittadino-straniero/visti/) website for more information about processing of visa applications.

## Visa Fees

[ita > phl > en > Table:Seafarer- Transit ( C ) > Visa Fees 1]
- In addition to the fees paid for the visa, an additional VFS charge of PHP 1933 (including VAT) is applied for each visa application.
- All Fees would be collected at the application submission counter by CASH and Card Payments .

Note: In case of failure to grant the visa, the amount paid is not under any circumstances refundable. 
- Applicants must pay a visa fee for the equivalent of € 90 in local currency Philippine Peso at the exchange rate set by the Embassy that can change periodically.

## Documents Required
Please check the following link to understand the documentation required for Seafarer-Transit (C)
Checklist for the documents (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5sfLKAfgTFfMNQ0obRe0CB/4ab40eea78c64d410b54c15540d41e33/SEAFARER_TRANSIT.pdf)
List of requirements for maritime agency accreditation (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2iWJFIiZhxKC1ZA51GfrTV/c95fa5295258e87ee76f8d489b32d120/list-of-schengen-insurance-companies-maritime.pdf)
Reminders for Preparing an Application

## Download Forms
SCHENGEN VISA APPLICAATION FOR English
SCHENGEN VISA APPLICAATION FORM  Italian

visa_types.visa_fees:
[ita > phl > en > Table:Tourist Visa (C) > Visa Fees 1]
- In addition to the fees paid for the visa, an additional VFS charge of PHP 1933 (including VAT) is applied for each visa application.
- All Fees would be collected at the application submission counter by CASH and Card Payments.

Note: In case of failure to grant the visa, the amount paid is not under any circumstances refundable. 
- Applicants must pay a visa fee for the equivalent of Euro 90 in local currency Philippine Peso at the exchange rate set by the Embassy that can change periodically.
- For children between six and twelve years of age, the visa fee is 45 Euro.
- The visa fee shall be waived for applicants belonging to one of the following categories:
- children under six years of age;

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Tourist visa is granted for those where the intention is to visit Italy for tourism and sightseeing.
(Maximum duration of stay: 90 days)
Applicants falling under this category must book an appointment to submit their visa application and have their biometric data registered at VFS Visa Application Center on their chosen appointment date and time.
BIOMETRIC DATA 
Following the entry into force of Decree-Law 145/2024, as of 11 January 2025, it will be mandatory to collect the fingerprints of applicants for both Schengen visas (type C) and national entry visas (type D). Applicants will therefore be required to appear in person when submitting their visa application. This is a simple procedure that takes only a few minutes. Since biometric data will be stored for 59 months, applicants will not need to provide fingerprints again for any subsequent visa applications, and it will not be necessary to apply in person for future visa requests.
Exemptions from the obligation to provide fingerprints are granted to a limited number of applicants: children under the age of 12 and individuals for whom fingerprint collection is physically impossible. Heads of State, government members, and accompanying delegation members, as well as spouses, are also exempted when attending official visits.
PROCESSING TIMES FOR VISA APPLICATIONS
Schengen regulations provide for a maximum processing time of 15 days, which can be extended to 30 days if necessary (Art. 23 of the Schengen Visa Code), while for national visas processing times can be extended up to 30 days for family and employment visas, and up to 120 days for self-employment. The Italian Embassy in Manila is committed to processing each visa application as quickly as possible
Please note that, especially during the period from February to September, applicants are strongly advised to observe the minimum timeframe of 15 calendar days between the submission of the application and the intended date of travel, as applications submitted with a shorter notice may not be processed in time.
Applications may be submitted as early as 180 days before the intended date of travel (270 days for seafarers).
Additional processing may apply to applicants who are nationals of countries subject to security consultations.
You may visit the Italian Embassy in Manila (https://ambmanila.esteri.it/en/servizi-consolari-e-visti/servizi-per-il-cittadino-straniero/visti/) website for more information about processing of visa applications.

Overview
This visa allows an applicant to enter Italy to study in Italian universities or other eligible institutions/schools or for professional trainings for a short period of stay.
(Maximum duration of stay: 90 days)
Applicants falling under this category must book an appointment to submit their visa application and have their biometric data registered at VFS Visa Application Center on their chosen appointment date and time.

Heads of State, government members, and accompanying delegation members, as well as spouses, are also exempted when attending official visits.

SCHENGEN VISA APPLICATION FORM ENG
SCHENGEN VISA APPLICATION FORM ITA
visa_types.overview:
Overview
- The visa of Mission is granted to the foreign national who, for reasons linked to his/her function in politics, government or public utility, has to travel to Italy.
- Therefore, foreign nationals who hold government posts or who are employees of public administration, public institutions or international organizations, and sent to Italy for reasons related to their functions, have access to such visa.
- For Missions of 90 days, download the request form of the Schengen Visa, C, and provide the following documents.
- For Missions of more than 90 days, download the request form of the National Visa, D, and provide the following documents.
BIOMETRIC DATA 
Following the entry into force of Decree-Law 145/2024, as of 11 January 2025, it will be mandatory to collect the fingerprints of applicants for both Schengen visas (type C) and national entry visas (type D). Applicants will therefore be required to appear in person when submitting their visa application. This is a simple procedure that takes only a few minutes. Since biometric data will be stored for 59 months, applicants will not need to provide fingerprints again for any subsequent visa applications, and it will not be necessary to apply in person for future visa requests.
Exemptions from the obligation to provide fingerprints are granted to a limited number of applicants: children under the age of 12 and individuals for whom fingerprint collection is physically impossible. Heads of State, government members, and accompanying delegation members, as well as spouses, are also exempted when attending official visits.
PROCESSING TIMES FOR VISA APPLICATIONS
Schengen regulations provide for a visa processing time of 15 calendar days, which may be extended up to 30 days if necessary (Art. 23 of the Schengen Visa Code). For national visas, processing time may extend to 30 days for family and employment purposes, and up to 120 days for self-employment. The Embassy of Italy in Manila is committed to processing each visa application as efficiently as possible.
Please note that, especially during the period from February to September, applicants are strongly advised to observe the minimum timeframe of 15 calendar days between the submission of the application and the intended date of travel, as applications submitted with a shorter notice may not be processed in time.
Applications may be submitted as early as 180 days before the intended date of travel (270 days for seafarers).
Additional processing may apply to applicants who are nationals of countries subject to security consultations.
You may visit the Italian Embassy in Manila (https://ambmanila.esteri.it/en/servizi-consolari-e-visti/servizi-per-il-cittadino-straniero/visti/) website for more information about processing of visa applications.

Exemptions from the obligation to provide fingerprints are granted to a limited number of applicants: children under the age of 12 and individuals for whom fingerprint collection is physically impossible. Heads of State, government members, and accompanying delegation members, as well as spouses, are also exempted when attending official visits.
PROCESSING TIMES FOR VISA APPLICATIONS
- Schengen regulations provide for a visa processing time of 15 calendar days, which may be extended up to 30 days if necessary (Art. 23 of the Schengen Visa Code). For national visas, processing time may extend to 30 days for family and employment purposes, and up to 120 days for self-employment. The Embassy of Italy in Manila is committed to processing each visa application as efficiently as possible.
Please note that, especially during the period from February to September, applicants are strongly advised to observe the minimum timeframe of 15 calendar days between the submission of the application and the intended date of travel, as applications submitted with a shorter notice may not be processed in time.
Applications may be submitted as early as 180 days before the intended date of travel (270 days for seafarers).Additional processing may apply to applicants who are nationals of countries subject to security consultations.
You may visit the Italian Embassy in Manila (https://ambmanila.esteri.it/en/servizi-consolari-e-visti/servizi-per-il-cittadino-straniero/visti/) website for more information about processing of visa applications.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Religious Purpose visa is granted for those applicants, invited by religious organizations, to visit Italy for religious purposes.
(Maximum duration of stay: 90 days)
Applicants falling under this category must book an appointment to submit their visa application and have their biometric data registered at VFS Visa Application Center on their chosen appointment date and time.
BIOMETRIC DATA 
Following the entry into force of Decree-Law 145/2024, as of 11 January 2025, it will be mandatory to collect the fingerprints of applicants for both Schengen visas (type C) and national entry visas (type D). Applicants will therefore be required to appear in person when submitting their visa application. This is a simple procedure that takes only a few minutes. Since biometric data will be stored for 59 months, applicants will not need to provide fingerprints again for any subsequent visa applications, and it will not be necessary to apply in person for future visa requests.
Exemptions from the obligation to provide fingerprints are granted to a limited number of applicants: children under the age of 12 and individuals for whom fingerprint collection is physically impossible. Heads of State, government members, and accompanying delegation members, as well as spouses, are also exempted when attending official visits.
PROCESSING TIMES FOR VISA APPLICATIONS
- Schengen regulations provide for a visa processing time of 15 calendar days, which may be extended up to 30 days if necessary (Art. 23 of the Schengen Visa Code). For national visas, processing time may extend to 30 days for family and employment purposes, and up to 120 days for self-employment. The Embassy of Italy in Manila is committed to processing each visa application as efficiently as possible.
Please note that, especially during the period from February to September, applicants are strongly advised to observe the minimum timeframe of 15 calendar days between the submission of the application and the intended date of travel, as applications submitted with a shorter notice may not be processed in time.
Applications may be submitted as early as 180 days before the intended date of travel (270 days for seafarers).Additional processing may apply to applicants who are nationals of countries subject to security consultations.
You may visit the Italian Embassy in Manila (https://ambmanila.esteri.it/en/servizi-consolari-e-visti/servizi-per-il-cittadino-straniero/visti/) website for more information about processing of visa applications.

## Visa Fees

[ita > phl > en > Table:Religious Purposes (C) > Visa Fees 1]
- In addition to the fees paid for the visa, an additional VFS charge of PHP 1933 (including VAT) is applied for each visa application.
- All Fees would be collected at the application submission counter by CASH and Card Payments 

Note: In case of failure to grant the visa, the amount paid is not under any circumstances refundable. 
- Applicants must pay a visa fee for the equivalent of € 90 in local currency Philippine Peso at the exchange rate set by the Embassy that can change periodically.

## Documents Required
Please check the following link to understand the documentation required for the Italy Religious Purposes Visa.
Checklist for the documents
List of Accredited Insurance Companies (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/50UP846ZvHF7eiO4rmmQgz/22cb2ed4b19c6027ccda4b7edf6f8d04/List-of-Schengen-Insurance-Companies.pdf)
Reminders for Preparing an Application

## Download Forms
SCHENGEN VISA APPLICATION FORM ENG
SCHENGEN VISA APPLICATION FORM ITA

visa_types.overview:
Overview
In favor of the spouse/child (also of the spouse)/parent of the Italian citizen, having verified the kinship/spouse bond, may, on the other hand, be issued a short-term Schengen visa (up to 90 days, type C) for tourism, with multiple entries. This visa is, therefore, intended to allow the foreign national to enter Italy, in case he needs an entry visa, for the sole purpose of crossing the external border of the Schengen space.
BIOMETRIC DATA 
Following the entry into force of Decree-Law 145/2024, as of 11 January 2025, it will be mandatory to collect the fingerprints of applicants for both Schengen visas (type C) and national entry visas (type D). Applicants will therefore be required to appear in person when submitting their visa application. This is a simple procedure that takes only a few minutes. Since biometric data will be stored for 59 months, applicants will not need to provide fingerprints again for any subsequent visa applications, and it will not be necessary to apply in person for future visa requests.
Exemptions from the obligation to provide fingerprints are granted to a limited number of applicants: children under the age of 12 and individuals for whom fingerprint collection is physically impossible. Heads of State, government members, and accompanying delegation members, as well as spouses, are also exempted when attending official visits.
PROCESSING TIMES FOR VISA APPLICATIONS
Schengen regulations provide for a visa processing time of 15 calendar days, which may be extended up to 30 days if necessary (Art. 23 of the Schengen Visa Code). For national visas, processing time may extend to 30 days for family and employment purposes, and up to 120 days for self-employment. The Embassy of Italy in Manila is committed to processing each visa application as efficiently as possible.
Please note that, especially during the period from February to September, applicants are strongly advised to observe the minimum timeframe of 15 calendar days between the submission of the application and the intended date of travel, as applications submitted with a shorter notice may not be processed in time.
Applications may be submitted as early as 180 days before the intended date of travel (270 days for seafarers).
Additional processing may apply to applicants who are nationals of countries subject to security consultations.
You may visit the Italian Embassy in Manila (https://ambmanila.esteri.it/en/servizi-consolari-e-visti/servizi-per-il-cittadino-straniero/visti/) website for more information about processing of visa applications.

The Embassy of Italy in Manila is committed to processing each visa application as efficiently as possible.
Please note that, especially during the period from February to September, applicants are strongly advised to observe the minimum timeframe of 15 calendar days between the submission of the application and the intended date of travel, as applications submitted with a shorter notice may not be processed in time.
Applications may be submitted as early as 180 days before the intended date of travel (270 days for seafarers).Additional processing may apply to applicants who are nationals of countries subject to security consultations.
You may visit the Italian Embassy in Manila (https://ambmanila.esteri.it/en/servizi-consolari-e-visti/servizi-per-il-cittadino-straniero/visti/) website for more information about processing of visa applications.

## Visa Fees

[ita > phl > en > Table:Subordinate Employment (C) > Visa Fees 1]
- In addition to the fees paid for the visa, an additional VFS charge of PHP 1933 (including VAT) is applied for each visa application.
- All Fees would be collected at the application submission counter by CASH and Card Payments 

Note: In case of failure to grant the visa, the amount paid is not under any circumstances refundable. 
- Applicants must pay a visa fee for the equivalent of € 90 in local currency Philippine Peso at the exchange rate set by the Embassy that can change periodically.
